mechanism.economic_hypothesis: An upward same-quarter revision in the Atlanta Fed GDPNow live estimate reflects improving aggregate growth information and favors SPY exposure for the following month.

signal.latest_row: latest forecast date no later than cutoff
signal.previous_row: immediately preceding row for the same forecast target quarter
signal.formula: latest GDP Nowcast minus previous same-quarter GDP Nowcast
signal.risk_on_rule: revision impulse strictly greater than zero
signal.availability: date-only historical row conservatively available at 23:59:59.999999 America/New_York on forecast date

portfolio.risk_on: 100 percent SPY subject to whole shares and cash

timing.decision: 08:30 America/New_York on the first accepted XNYS session after signal month end
timing.execution: same accepted session retrospective raw open
timing.decision_price: preceding accepted session retrospective raw close
timing.holding: until next monthly accepted execution open
timing.drawdown_observations: monthly execution-open and terminal-open NAV only

segments.boundary_gap: 2021-11 signal and 2021-12 execution are unused; no state or portfolio carries across segments

comparators.spy_buyhold: buy SPY once at the segment first execution open, never rebalance, retain distributions as zero-return cash, measure at terminal open
comparators.monthly_50_50: rebalance monthly to 50 percent SPY and 50 percent zero-return cash using identical opens, whole shares and cost contract

corporate_actions.source: State Street official SPY distribution history
corporate_actions.rule: apply each event exactly once on ex-date to freeze session-open share entitlement; settle pending cash on official pay date

gates_per_segment: at least 12 SPY and at least 12 cash months
strategy terminal wealth strictly above monthly 50/50 comparator
strategy arithmetic mean monthly return strictly above monthly 50/50 comparator
strategy monthly maximum drawdown no worse than SPY buy-and-hold
accepted_limitations: GDPNow historical forecast timestamps are date-only and are not same-day tradable evidence
the two archived weekend-date anomalies precede validation and secondary segments
State Street distributions are retrospective account-reconstruction inputs rather than signal evidence
all historical results through 2026-06-30 are exploratory and nonconfirmatory
